query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "minimax sparse attention" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (d) as it proposes a specific sparse attention mechanism (Gated Sparse Attention) that directly extends the domain of "MiniMax Sparse Attention" by addressing its complementary weaknesses (training stability and attention sinks) while maintaining computational efficiency. It serves as a canonical methodological reference for the specific sub-field of sparse attention architectures in long-context LLMs.
